name: Stream Architecture
description: Real-time updates via CRDT - subscribe to data changes as they happen

content.title: Stream-Based Architecture
content.content:
# Stream-Based Architecture

MeshWeaver provides data as streams - subscribe once and receive updates as they happen. No polling, no stale data.

## Everything is a Stream

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Client/Agent
    participant Mesh as MeshWeaver
    participant Source as Data Source

    Client->>Mesh: Subscribe to @app/Claims/ActiveClaims
    Mesh->>Source: Watch for changes
    Mesh->>Client: Initial data snapshot

    Note over Source: Claim updated

    Source->>Mesh: Change notification
    Mesh->>Client: Stream update (delta)

    Note over Client: UI/Agent receives update automatically
```

## Subscribe, Don't Poll

| Traditional Approach | Stream Approach |
|---------------------|----------------|
| Poll every N seconds | Subscribe once |
| Full refresh each time | Receive deltas |
| Wasted API calls | Updates on change |
| Always somewhat stale | Always current |

## CRDT for Consistency

MeshWeaver uses **Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDT)** for distributed consistency:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Change in Source] --> B[MeshWeaver]
    B --> C[Subscriber 1]
    B --> D[Subscriber 2]
    B --> E[Subscriber 3]
    
    C --> F[Consistent View]
    D --> F
    E --> F
```

**CRDT Properties:**
- Multiple clients can subscribe to the same data
- Updates propagate automatically
- No central locking required
- Eventually consistent across all subscribers

## Real-Time Use Cases

| Use Case | How Streams Help |
|----------|------------------|
| **Dashboard updates** | KPIs refresh automatically when data changes |
| **Collaboration** | Multiple users see changes in real-time |
| **AI monitoring** | Agents react to new data immediately |
| **Notifications** | Alerts triggered on specific changes |

## Event-Driven Integration

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "MeshWeaver"
        A[Stream Engine]
    end

    subgraph "Message Bus Options"
        B[Change Feed]
        C[Service Bus]
        D[Event Hubs]
    end

    subgraph "Consumers"
        E[Downstream System]
        F[Analytics]
        G[Notifications]
    end

    A --> B --> E
    A --> C --> F
    A --> D --> G
```

## Integration Patterns

| Pattern | Description | Use Case |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **Publish** | Emit events on data change | Notify downstream systems |
| **Subscribe** | React to external events | Process incoming documents |
| **Request/Reply** | Synchronous query | API queries |
| **Saga** | Multi-step workflows | Complex business processes |

## Benefits

- **Real-time**: See changes as they happen
- **Efficient**: Only transfer what changed (deltas)
- **Scalable**: Pub/sub handles many subscribers
- **Decoupled**: Producers and consumers independent
- **Reactive**: Build responsive applications
